words.definition: to guess or think about something without knowing for sure
words.example: I speculate that tomorrow will be sunny because the sky is clear tonight.
words.thinkOfIt: like guessing what's in a wrapped present before you open it

words.definition: to think about something carefully for a long time
words.example: She sat by the window to ponder which sport to choose for PE.
words.thinkOfIt: like staring at a chess board trying to plan your next move

words.definition: to believe something is true without checking it first
words.example: I assume the bus will be on time, but I should check the app.
words.thinkOfIt: like thinking your mum made dinner without looking in the kitchen

words.definition: to suddenly understand or become aware of something
words.example: I realise now that I left my book at school.
words.thinkOfIt: like a lightbulb switching on inside your head

words.definition: to remember something from before
words.example: I can recall exactly what we had for dinner last Sunday.
words.thinkOfIt: like pulling a memory out of a drawer in your mind

quickCheck.question: Which word means 'to guess without knowing for sure'?
Which word means 'to think about something carefully for a long time'?
Which word means 'to believe something without checking'?
Which word means 'to suddenly understand'?
Which word means 'to remember something'?

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Size & Amount Words
lessons.theme: Words about how big or how much

words.definition: more than enough of something
words.example: There were abundant apples on the tree, enough for the whole class.
words.thinkOfIt: like a bowl so full of sweets it's about to overflow

words.definition: to get smaller or less
words.example: The ice cream began to diminish as we all took big scoops.
words.thinkOfIt: like a bar of soap shrinking each time you use it

words.definition: extremely big
words.example: The blue whale is an immense animal, bigger than a bus.
words.thinkOfIt: like a mountain that you can't see the top of

words.definition: hard to find because there isn't much of it
words.example: Water is scarce in the desert, so every drop matters.
words.thinkOfIt: like the last piece of cake that everyone wants

words.definition: enough for what you need
words.example: One sandwich is sufficient for my lunch today.
words.thinkOfIt: like a cup filled exactly to the line, not more, not less

quickCheck.question: Which word means 'more than enough'?
Which word means 'to get smaller'?
Which word means 'extremely big'?
Which word means 'hard to find, not much of it'?
Which word means 'enough for what you need'?

words.definition: not wanting to do something
words.example: Jacob was reluctant to get out of bed on the cold morning.
words.thinkOfIt: like dragging your feet when you're told to tidy your room

words.definition: happy and calm with what you have
words.example: She was content reading her book in the garden.
words.thinkOfIt: like a cat curled up in a sunny spot

words.definition: worried and nervous about something
words.example: He felt anxious before the spelling test.
words.thinkOfIt: like butterflies fluttering around in your tummy

words.definition: very happy about something
words.example: Jacob was delighted when he got a new LEGO set.
words.thinkOfIt: like the feeling when you open the perfect birthday present

words.definition: extremely angry
words.example: Mum was furious when the dog ate her dinner.
words.thinkOfIt: like a kettle whistling at full steam

quickCheck.question: Which word means 'not wanting to do something'?
Which word means 'happy and calm with what you have'?
Which word means 'worried and nervous'?
Which word means 'very happy'?
Which word means 'extremely angry'?

lessons.title: Lesson 4: Actions & Movement Words
lessons.theme: Words about how people and things move or act

words.definition: to run very fast for a short distance
words.example: He had to dash to catch the bus before it left.
words.thinkOfIt: like sprinting across the playground when the bell rings

words.definition: to trip or nearly fall over while walking
words.example: I tried not to stumble over the toy car in the hallway.
words.thinkOfIt: like tripping on a rug and catching yourself just in time

words.definition: to throw something hard and fast
words.example: The pitcher will hurl the ball towards the batter.
words.thinkOfIt: like chucking a snowball at a tree as hard as you can

words.definition: to move slowly and quietly so nobody notices
words.example: The cat began to creep up on the sleeping bird.
words.thinkOfIt: like tiptoeing downstairs on Christmas morning

words.definition: to grab something quickly and firmly
words.example: She had to seize the rope before it slipped away.
words.thinkOfIt: like catching a balloon before it floats away

quickCheck.question: Which word means 'to run very fast for a short distance'?
Which word means 'to trip while walking'?
Which word means 'to throw hard and fast'?
Which word means 'to move slowly and quietly'?
Which word means 'to grab something quickly and firmly'?

lessons.title: Lesson 5: Describing People Words
lessons.theme: Words that describe what people are like

words.definition: very careful and paying attention to every tiny detail
words.example: Jacob is meticulous when he builds his LEGO models.
words.thinkOfIt: like checking every single piece is straight before moving on

words.definition: willing to share and give lots to others
words.example: Grandma is generous and always shares her biscuits.
words.thinkOfIt: like always offering the biggest slice of cake to a friend

words.definition: shy and easily frightened
words.example: The timid puppy hid behind the sofa when visitors arrived.
words.thinkOfIt: like a mouse peeping out from behind a cupboard

words.definition: not willing to change your mind
words.example: My brother is stubborn and always wants the blue cup.
words.thinkOfIt: like a donkey that won't budge no matter how hard you pull

words.definition: clever in a sneaky, tricky way
words.example: The cunning fox tricked the farmer and took the chicken.
words.thinkOfIt: like a fox planning a clever plan to get dinner

quickCheck.question: Which word means 'very careful with every tiny detail'?
Which word means 'willing to share and give'?
Which word means 'shy and easily frightened'?
Which word means 'not willing to change your mind'?
Which word means 'clever in a sneaky way'?

words.definition: extremely old, from a very long time ago
words.example: We learned about ancient Egypt and the pyramids in school.
words.thinkOfIt: like a fossil that's been in the ground for millions of years

words.definition: lasting only a short time
words.example: We had a brief chat before class started.
words.thinkOfIt: like a blink, over before you notice

words.definition: happening in the end, after a long time
words.example: After many tries, her eventual victory made her smile.
words.thinkOfIt: like finally reaching the end of a really long level

words.definition: done quickly without delay
words.example: She sent a prompt reply to my text within a minute.
words.thinkOfIt: like answering the door as soon as the bell rings

words.definition: happening often, again and again
words.example: Jacob's frequent visits to the library helped him read lots of books.
words.thinkOfIt: like a bus that comes every few minutes

quickCheck.question: Which word means 'extremely old'?
Which word means 'lasting only a short time'?
Which word means 'happening in the end'?
Which word means 'done quickly without delay'?
Which word means 'happening often'?

lessons.title: Lesson 7: Cause & Effect Words
lessons.theme: Words about what makes things happen

words.definition: causing terrible damage or sadness
words.example: The devastating storm knocked down trees all along the street.
words.thinkOfIt: like a tornado leaving a flattened trail behind it

words.definition: the power to change how someone thinks or acts
words.example: Jacob's teacher has a good influence on his reading habits.
words.thinkOfIt: like a magnet pulling metal towards it

words.definition: to make something happen straight away
words.example: Loud noises can trigger a fire alarm to start ringing.
words.thinkOfIt: like pressing a button that instantly starts a machine

words.definition: something that happens as a result of something else
words.example: The consequence of staying up late is being tired the next day.
words.thinkOfIt: like dominoes falling: push one and the rest follow

words.definition: to stop something from happening
words.example: Wearing a helmet can prevent a bump on the head.
words.thinkOfIt: like putting up an umbrella before the rain starts

quickCheck.question: Which word means 'causing terrible damage'?
Which word means 'the power to change how someone acts'?
Which word means 'to make something happen straight away'?
Which word means 'something that happens as a result'?
Which word means 'to stop something from happening'?

lessons.title: Lesson 8: Formal & Fancy Words
lessons.theme: Grown-up words that mean simple things

words.definition: having lots of detail and carefully planned parts
words.example: She drew an elaborate map with every room in the castle.
words.thinkOfIt: like a birthday cake with loads of icing, sprinkles and decorations

words.definition: to start or begin something
words.example: The lesson will commence as soon as everyone sits down.
words.thinkOfIt: like the starter flag dropping at the start of a race

words.definition: to buy something
words.example: Dad went to purchase some milk from the shop.
words.thinkOfIt: like swapping money for something you want in a shop

words.definition: to live in a place
words.example: We reside in a house near the park.
words.thinkOfIt: like marking your house on a map: that's where you reside

words.definition: to watch something carefully
words.example: The scientist will observe the ants for an hour.
words.thinkOfIt: like a detective watching everything that happens

quickCheck.question: Which word means 'having lots of detail'?
Which word means 'to start'?
Which word means 'to buy something'?
Which word means 'to live in a place'?
Which word means 'to watch carefully'?

lessons.title: Lesson 9: Problem & Solution Words
lessons.theme: Words about tricky situations and fixing them

words.definition: a tough choice where both options are hard
words.example: I had a dilemma: watch the match or finish my homework.
words.thinkOfIt: like choosing between two ice cream flavours you both love

words.definition: to find an answer to a problem
words.example: We had to resolve the argument about whose turn it was.
words.thinkOfIt: like fitting the last piece of a jigsaw puzzle

words.definition: something in the way that stops you
words.example: The fallen tree was an obstacle on the running path.
words.thinkOfIt: like a wall blocking the door you want to walk through

words.definition: to beat a problem or difficulty
words.example: Jacob worked hard to overcome his fear of swimming.
words.thinkOfIt: like climbing over a wall that was blocking your way

words.definition: a plan for how to do something well
words.example: My strategy for the test is to read every question twice.
words.thinkOfIt: like the plan you use to win at chess

quickCheck.question: Which word means 'a tough choice where both options are hard'?
Which word means 'to find an answer to a problem'?
Which word means 'something in the way'?
Which word means 'to beat a problem'?
Which word means 'a plan for how to do something'?

lessons.title: Lesson 10: Positive & Negative Words
lessons.theme: Words for good and not-so-good things

words.definition: very determined and not giving up
words.example: She was resolute about finishing the race, even with a sore knee.
words.thinkOfIt: like a tree standing firm in a strong wind

words.definition: watching carefully for trouble or danger
words.example: The lifeguard was vigilant as he watched the swimmers.
words.thinkOfIt: like an owl keeping a close eye on the forest at night

words.definition: weak and without much strength
words.example: His feeble attempt to lift the box made everyone laugh kindly.
words.thinkOfIt: like a twig that snaps as soon as you bend it

words.definition: very impressive and wonderful
words.example: We had a splendid day at the seaside with ice cream and sandcastles.
words.thinkOfIt: like fireworks lighting up the whole sky

words.definition: very bad or unpleasant
words.example: The weather was dreadful, so we stayed inside all day.
words.thinkOfIt: like a rainy day when your shoes get soaked

quickCheck.question: Which word means 'very determined and not giving up'?
Which word means 'watching carefully for danger'?
Which word means 'weak and without much strength'?
Which word means 'very impressive and wonderful'?
Which word means 'very bad or unpleasant'?
